```json
{
  "diagnosis": "This job exhibits severe small I/O access patterns with 102,400 write operations averaging only 4KB per call, resulting in extremely poor bandwidth of 2.8 MB/s. The 204,000 unaligned file accesses and 204,800 total access operations indicate excessive syscall overhead from tiny transfers. With 419MB written over 78 seconds using 4KB chunks, the job is spending most time in kernel transitions rather than actual data movement.",
  
  "recommendations": [
    {
      "priority": 1,
      "bottleneck_dimension": "access_granularity", 
      "explanation": "The job performs 102,400 writes of 4KB each instead of using larger transfer sizes. This creates massive syscall overhead - each 4KB write requires a kernel transition. The benchmark evidence shows identical small transfer patterns can be fixed by buffering writes into >=1MB chunks.",
      "code_before": "for (i=0; i<102400; i++) {\n    write(fd, buffer, 4096);  /* 4KB per syscall */\n}",
      "code_after": "char *big_buf = malloc(1048576);  /* 1MB buffer */\nsize_t offset = 0;\nfor (i=0; i<102400; i++) {\n    memcpy(big_buf + offset, buffer, 4096);\n    offset += 4096;\n    if (offset >= 1048576) {\n        write(fd, big_buf, offset);  /* 1MB per syscall */\n        offset = 0;\n    }\n}\nif (offset > 0) write(fd, big_buf, offset);  /* flush remainder */",
      "expected_speedup": "10-50x based on reducing syscall count from 102,400 to ~400",
      "kb_citation": "ior_ior_small_direct_t4096_n4_r3_38",
      "confidence": "high",
      "api_change": "POSIX write with buffering strategy"
    },
    {
      "priority": 2, 
      "bottleneck_dimension": "access_granularity",
      "explanation": "Alternative MPI-IO collective approach for the same small transfer problem. Since this appears to be a parallel job (4 processes), collective I/O can aggregate transfers across processes and reduce per-process syscall overhead.",
      "code_before": "/* Per-process independent writes */\nfor (i=0; i<102400; i++) {\n    MPI_File_write(fh, buffer, 4096, MPI_BYTE, &status);\n}",
      "code_after": "/* Collective write with larger datatype */\nMPI_Datatype large_type;\nMPI_Type_contiguous(262144, MPI_BYTE, &large_type);  /* 1MB type */\nMPI_Type_commit(&large_type);\nfor (i=0; i<400; i++) {  /* 400 collective ops vs 102400 individual */\n    MPI_File_write_all(fh, big_buffer, 1, large_type, &status);\n}",
      "expected_speedup": "5-20x based on collective I/O reducing coordination overhead",
      "kb_citation": "ior_ior_small_direct_t4096_n4_r2_37", 
      "confidence": "medium",
      "api_change": "POSIX write -> MPI_File_write_all with large datatypes"
    }
  ],
  
  "overall_expected_improvement": "10-50x speedup range, potentially achieving 28-140 MB/s vs current 2.8 MB/s"
}
```
system_prompt:
You are an HPC I/O performance expert. You analyze Darshan profiling data
and provide specific, actionable code-level optimization recommendations.

RULES:
1. Every recommendation MUST be grounded in the benchmark evidence provided.
2. Include specific before/after code snippets showing the exact change.
3. Cite the KB entry ID for each recommendation.
4. Quantify expected improvement using benchmark measurements only.
5. Do NOT fabricate performance numbers or API calls that don't exist.
6. Prioritize recommendations by expected impact (highest speedup first).
7. Use standard HPC I/O APIs: POSIX (read/write), MPI-IO (MPI_File_*), HDF5 (H5D*).
user_prompt:
Analyze this HPC job's I/O behavior and provide code-level optimization recommendations.

## Detected Bottlenecks (ML classifier, Micro-F1=0.923):
  - **access_granularity** (confidence=1.00): I/O operations with very small transfer sizes (<1MB), causing excessive syscall overhead

## Key Contributing Features (SHAP per-label attribution):

  access_granularity:
    - POSIX_FILE_NOT_ALIGNED = 204000.0000 (|SHAP|=3.8318, increases_risk)
    - POSIX_ACCESS1_COUNT = 204800.0000 (|SHAP|=1.9607, increases_risk)
    - POSIX_WRITES = 102400.0000 (|SHAP|=1.4032, increases_risk)
    - has_apmpi = 0.0000 (|SHAP|=1.2120, decreases_risk)
    - POSIX_CONSEC_READS = 102396.0000 (|SHAP|=0.7958, increases_risk)


## Benchmark Evidence (verified Knowledge Base with source code):

  --- KB Entry 1 (ID: ior_ior_small_direct_t4096_n4_r3_38) ---
  Benchmark: ior | Scenario: ior_small_direct_t4096_n4_r3
  Bottleneck: access_granularity
  Source: https://github.com/hpc/ior (C)
  Code (POSIX): write(fd, buf, transferSize)  /* src/aiori-POSIX.c:250 */
  Code (MPIIO_indep): MPI_File_write(fh, buf, transferSize, MPI_BYTE, &status)  /* src/aiori-MPIIO.c:200 */
  Cause: I/O operations with very small transfer sizes (<1MB), causing excessive system call overhead
  Fix: Increase transfer/buffer size to >=1MB. For POSIX: aggregate small writes into large buffers. For MPI-IO: use collective I/O with larger datatypes.
  Code BEFORE:
    for (i=0; i<n; i++) write(fd, buf, 64);  /* 64B per call */
  Code AFTER:
    /* Buffer small writes, flush in large chunks */
memcpy(big_buf + offset, buf, 64); offset += 64;
if (offset >= 1048576) { write(fd, big_buf, offset); offset = 0; }

  --- KB Entry 2 (ID: ior_ior_small_direct_t4096_n4_r2_37) ---
  Benchmark: ior | Scenario: ior_small_direct_t4096_n4_r2
  Bottleneck: access_granularity
  Source: https://github.com/hpc/ior (C)
  Code (POSIX): write(fd, buf, transferSize)  /* src/aiori-POSIX.c:250 */
  Code (MPIIO_indep): MPI_File_write(fh, buf, transferSize, MPI_BYTE, &status)  /* src/aiori-MPIIO.c:200 */
  Cause: I/O operations with very small transfer sizes (<1MB), causing excessive system call overhead
  Fix: Increase transfer/buffer size to >=1MB. For POSIX: aggregate small writes into large buffers. For MPI-IO: use collective I/O with larger datatypes.
  Code BEFORE:
    for (i=0; i<n; i++) write(fd, buf, 64);  /* 64B per call */
  Code AFTER:
    /* Buffer small writes, flush in large chunks */
memcpy(big_buf + offset, buf, 64); offset += 64;
if (offset >= 1048576) { write(fd, big_buf, offset); offset = 0; }

  --- KB Entry 3 (ID: ior_ior_small_direct_t4096_n4_r3_93) ---
  Benchmark: ior | Scenario: ior_small_direct_t4096_n4_r3
  Bottleneck: access_granularity
  Source: https://github.com/hpc/ior (C)
  Code (POSIX): write(fd, buf, transferSize)  /* src/aiori-POSIX.c:250 */
  Code (MPIIO_indep): MPI_File_write(fh, buf, transferSize, MPI_BYTE, &status)  /* src/aiori-MPIIO.c:200 */
  Cause: I/O operations with very small transfer sizes (<1MB), causing excessive system call overhead
  Fix: Increase transfer/buffer size to >=1MB. For POSIX: aggregate small writes into large buffers. For MPI-IO: use collective I/O with larger datatypes.
  Code BEFORE:
    for (i=0; i<n; i++) write(fd, buf, 64);  /* 64B per call */
  Code AFTER:

memcpy(big_buf + offset, buf, 64); offset += 64;
if (offset >= 1048576) { write(fd, big_buf, offset); offset = 0; }


## Job Darshan Summary:
  nprocs: 4.0
